zh.lifeSummary: Jane Verdon於大約1385年出生在英國英格蘭。她和Thomas Flame至少有1個女兒。她去世於1485年享年101歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Jane Verdon于大约1385年出生在英国英格兰。她和Thomas Flame至少有1个女儿。她去世于1485年享年101岁。

nameMeanings.name: Verdon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Lucien, Marcel, Pierre, Renaud, Sylvie.
French: habitational name from (Le) Verdon, the name of several places in various parts of France, for example in Dordogne, Gironde, and Marne. Compare   Ferdon  .
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Verdun, the name of a fief in Vessey (Manche), France. Compare   Verdun  .

variants.name: Verdin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Ramon, Angel, Luis, Ruben, Azael, Fernando, Fidel, Francisco, Guillermo, Javier.
English (Lancashire and Cheshire): variant of   Verdon  .

variants.name: Ferdon
meanings: Altered form, under American Dutch influence in New Netherland, of French   Verdon  .

variants.name: Wearden
meanings: English (Lancashire): apparently from a placename with Old English denu ‘valley’ as the second element. However, the Victoria County History for Staffordshire identifies John de Werdon, mentioned in 1280, as having his name from Verdun (Manche, France); see Verdon .

variants.name: Verdun
meanings: French: habitational name from any of several places in France called Verdun. The placename is probably of Gaulish origin, deriving from ver(n) ‘alder’ + dūn ‘hill, fortress’.
Spanish and Catalan: habitational name from Verdú in Catalonia (Spain), which has the same origin as the French name (see 1 above).
English (of Norman origin): variant of   Verdon  , a cognate of 1 above. This surname is very rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

en.lifeSummary: Jane Verdon was born about 1385, in England, United Kingdom. She had at least 1 daughter with Thomas Flame. She died in 1485, at the age of 101.

fr.lifeSummary: Jane Verdon est née environ 1385, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Elle a eu au moins 1 fille avec Thomas Flame. Elle est décédée en 1485, à l’âge de 101 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Jane Verdon wurde um 1385 in England, United Kingdom geboren. Sie hatte mit Thomas Flame mindestens 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1485 im Alter von 101 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Jane Verdon è nata il circa 1385 nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlia con Thomas Flame. Morì nel 1485, all'età di 101 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Jane Verdon(은)는 대략 1385년 영국 잉글랜드에서 태어났다. 그녀는 Thomas Flame(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1485년 101세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Jane Verdon nasceu aproximadamente 1385, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filha com Thomas Flame. Ela faleceu em 1485, com 101 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Jane Verdon родилась около 1385 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У неё была по крайней мере 1 дочь от Thomas Flame. Она умерла в 1485 г. в возрасте 101 год.
